programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Mac and Stella trail a killer around Brooklyn while investigating the murder of a man whose body was dumped on a subway track. Their only lead is a woman seen in a home movie in the victim's flat. Danny looks into the death of a gallery owner with links to the Mob, and the corpse of a building labourer with a foul-smelling wound poses problems for Aiden and Flack. Guest starring Kelly Hu

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Against his better judgement, Gibbs comes out of retirement to help Fornell and his daughter, whose lives are being threatened by an escaped convict. The team reopens the criminal's case and questions a former sailor, the only person to have contacted him while he was in jail. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette, with Hal Holbrook

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine Daniel Zuna is found dead in a pool of his own blood in an alleyway outside a night club, and the team's first stop is to investigate the mixed martial arts gym where the victim spent most of his off-duty time. Kensi poses as Zuna's new girlfriend and suspects the murder could be related to something that happened in Iraq, prompting Sam to go undercover as an MMA fighter in order to see if he can find out the truth. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J and Daniela Ruah

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: A serious error in judgement leads Kimble to snub a sheriff, and it all but leads to his undoing.

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Logan has to face up to his cultural biases when a Lebanese gunrunner and an Irish terrorist come under investigation over the murder of a drug smuggler

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on a joint Coast Guard and NCIS training exercise at sea with Agent Borin, Gibbs spots a dead man overboard, who is found to be a missing member of the crew of a nearby vessel. However, when the agent boards the ship for questioning, he realises it has been commandeered by pirates. Naval drama, starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Adventure with the modern-day lawman whose crime-solving methods are rooted firmly in the Old West. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. Ziva and Gibbs lead the mission to rescue the captured Marine in Afghanistan before discovering that her captor's brothers have bigger plans and are at large in America. Gibbs has to be stopped when he tries to employ methods similar to those used on Kinah and Lala in an effort to gain information, but Vance has a trick that gives the agents an idea of what the siblings have in mind

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A couple are taking in the view from a cliff top when a car driven by an already bleeding man crashes into theirs and teeters on the brink before plunging over the precipice. Callen and the team are brought in to investigate when the driver is identified as Lieutenant Brian Roth, and they discover that the car is registered to a Hollywood socialite Aubrey Darva. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators discover probationary agent Ned Dorneget has been bugged by Harper Dearing, the man behind a spate of arson attacks on US Navy vessels. A phone call from the man himself makes it clear he is out for revenge, and Sam Ryan has information as to the motive behind it. Guest starring Richard Schiff (The West Wing)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tries to track down wanted terrorist Harper Dearing before another US Navy ship is destroyed, and a desperate Gibbs enlists the aid of a prisoner to draw his target out of hiding. Meanwhile, Palmer is forced to consider putting his nuptials on hold as the stand-off with Dearing becomes heated. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The aftermath of Harper Dearing's bomb attack on the NCIS headquarters dominates proceedings. As rescue crews sift through the debris searching for survivors, Abby, Gibbs, McGee and Vance are accounted for, but Ziva and Tony are trapped in a lift. Dearing makes contact after his ex-wife is taken into custody, enabling his car to be traced to an abandoned lot - and the agents arrive just in time to see the vehicle blow up. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team investigate the death of an armoury employee when divers find her body in a car at the bottom of a lake. The victim had been reported missing since she was evacuated before the blast at NCIS headquarters. Meanwhile, crisis counsellor Miles Wolf carries out psychological assessments of all the agents in the wake of the bombings. Guest starring Steve Valentine (Crossing Jordan)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While on medical leave, Ducky reopens a 12-year-old case by talking officials at a cemetery into releasing the body of a navy commander whose post-mortem he carried out 12 years previously. Meanwhile, the team is called in to investigate the death of a sergeant from carbon monoxide poisoning - and finds a copy of Ducky's exhumation request on the man's laptop. Crime drama, starring David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is found on the side of a remote road and toxicology tests reveal he died of overhydration. When another man is found dead nearby, the NCIS team begins to suspect a serial killer is on the loose. Meanwhile, Ducky introduces Gibbs to his new girlfriend. Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part one of two. A military aircraft transporting the bodies of dead US marines back home crashes before reaching its destination, and Gibbs and the team are called in to help identify human remains at the site of the wreckage. However, a DNA discrepancy turns a routine investigation into a search for a missing person. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Naval intelligence ensign is killed in an unusual car accident. programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Special Agent Ned Dorneget of the NCIS cyber division joins the team to further their investigation into The Calling, the insidious terror network radicalising youngsters via the internet. When it emerges young suicide bomber Bradley was not, in fact, affiliated with The Calling, Gibbs and his colleagues realise they might be back to square-one. Determined to bring the organisation down, the agents pursue two new leads, an arms dealer who may be supplying The Calling with their signature German S-mines, and another boy given a deadly mission by the terrorists. Guest starring Matt Jones

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The death of Special Agent Ned Dorneget hits the team hard, and when Gibbs receives a visit from his late colleague's mother - CIA Officer Joanna Teague - he must decide whether to let her help with the investigation into the Calling. Meanwhile, Gibbs hopes his young charge Luke, formerly one of the Calling's radicalised footsoldiers, might be key to bringing the organisation down. However, when the terrorists discover where their defecting comrade has taken refuge, the abduct him and flee to Iraq. The team follows hot on their heels, but it seems Luke's ties to the Calling might be stronger than anyone at NCIS ever anticipated. Naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, with guest Mimi Rogers

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Tony and the team try to prove Ziva had nothing to do with a political assassination carried out by her former Mossad partner - but have to tackle the case without Gibbs, who has retired to Mexico and now spends his time building boats. Naval crime drama, starring Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Marine gunnery sergeant Wendell Dobbs is gunned down in a drive-by shooting by assailants wielding AK-47s, and the team suspects the victim may have been involved in illegally importing automatic weapons into the country and supplying them to local gangs. Sam goes undercover to find the truth after learning Dobbs gave up his combat post to mentor his younger brother, who had been hanging out with a gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

Guest starring Anita Dobson

programme.desc: Gerry is forced to confront his past when the team reinvestigates the 33-year-old murder of a doctor whose body was found hanging from a meat hook on a butcher's stall at Smithfield Market. The butcher had gone missing on the same day, and since then the community has rallied to help his widow keep the business afloat. However, his recently discovered remains reveal a match to an unidentified person and lead to a shocking revelation for Sandra. Guest starring Ken Farrington, David Calder and Diana Quick, with Amanda Redman and Alun Armstrong

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: The accidental fatal shooting of a Polish teenager in an armed police operation sparks a series of murders, which links Turkish gangsters, a grieving family and a fractured firearms unit. As the inquest delivers a lawful killings verdict, IPCC investigator Vicky Sharpe calls on the Lyell team to investigate the connection between the events and review the police evidence, but Sharpe's relationship with the family of the teenager and her suspicion of the firearms division lead Nikki and Thomas to distrust her motives. Feature-length episode, starring Emilia Fox

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor takes Martha back to present-day Earth, where she finds her sister working for an elderly scientist who has built a machine to reverse the ageing process. When he tests it on himself, his youth is restored, but the experiment goes wrong and he transforms into a ravenous monster that must feed on humans to survive. Guest starring Mark Gatiss and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: The Doctor and Martha face a desperate race against time to save the crew of a spaceship on course to crash into a star. The pair have only 42 minutes to prevent a collision - and their task is made even more difficult by crew members who have been possessed by a mysterious force. Michelle Collins (Coronation Street) guest stars alongside David Tennant and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. A teacher who works in an English public school in 1913 dreams of travelling through time and space in a mysterious blue box. It is not long before sinister events begin to unfold and his maid Martha tries to convince him only he can save the world. Guest starring Spaced's Jessica Hynes, with David Tennant and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Babs
programme.desc: Biopic of Barbara Windsor, following the key events of the actress' life over the course of 50 years. The story begins in 1993, with the actress preparing for a stage performance shortly before joining the cast of EastEnders. She looks back to her childhood as a lonely evacuee in the Second World War, her rise to fame in the Carry On films and her difficult relationships with her father and her first husband, gangster Ronnie Knight. Samantha Spiro, Jaime Winstone, Honor Kneafsey and Windsor herself share the lead role

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

programme.title: We're Doomed! The Dad's Army Story
programme.desc: Comedy drama based on the story of how Jimmy Perry and David Croft brought the sitcom Dad's Army to life. Running from Perry's initial idea in 1967 until the transmission of the first episode in 1968, the film shows the beginnings of their writing partnership and the casting woes, personal clashes and production difficulties that put the show's existence in jeopardy. Starring Paul Ritter and Richard Dormer

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Remake of the classic sitcom, featuring scripts associated with missing episodes performed by a new cast. Private Walker, source of the platoon's supplies of whisky, cigarettes and other essential black-market goods, is called up to serve in the regular Army, so Mainwaring decides to use all his standing as an officer and a bank manager to have him excused from military service. Meanwhile, Jones and the others try to ensure that Walker fails his medical. Kevin McNally stars as Captain Mainwaring, with Robert Bathurst as Sergeant Wilson, Kevin Eldon as Corporal Jones and Mathew Horne as Private Walker

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: When he's told he can make a member of the platoon a corporal, Mainwaring instigates a competition between Frazer and Jones to see who deserves the promotion. Both do their best to impress him, particularly during a demonstration on how to face down a Nazi stormtrooper, but things go awry when a power-crazed Frazer charges the other men with court-martial offences. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst, Kevin Eldon and David Hayman

programme.title: Dad's Army: The Lost Episodes
programme.desc: Tipped off by Frazer and Godfrey, Mainwaring and Wilson investigate a house from where they believe someone may have been signalling to enemy aircraft. They take the suspect back to the church hall, but chaos ensues when an incendiary bomb comes through the roof and the platoon desperately battles the blaze. Remake of the classic sitcom, starring Kevin McNally, Robert Bathurst and Kevin Eldon

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Norman the breadman fails to deliver, the stripper cannot play the accordion and Jean has bridesmaids' knickers on her mind. First episode of the canteen comedy, starring Victoria Wood, Julie Walters and Celia Imrie

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: The staff await an important royal visit, but first Stan has to pick just the right toupee, and Bren is determined to get the tables cleared away in time. Comedy, starring Victoria Wood and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: dinnerladies
programme.desc: Bren's mother wreaks havoc in the factory and incurs Stan's wrath by pursuing an ill-considered romance, while Anita organises a heated debate on the workers' current situation. Comedy, with Victoria Wood and Julie Walters

programme.title: The Two Ronnies
programme.desc: The two comedians host Christmas dinner, but someone steals the turkey and they must put their heads together to capture the thief. Comedy starring Ronnie Corbett and Ronnie Barker

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A new midwife joins the team as the area is rocked by power cuts and blizzards. The new arrival is delayed by the weather and falls ill as soon as she arrives. However, she is quickly dragged from her sick bed to assist with a difficult birth. Nurse Crane and Dr Turner treat an elderly cancer patient who faces losing her house due to slum clearances, and try to convince Sergeant Woolf to allow her to die at home

programme.title: My Life Is Murder
programme.desc: After a near-death experience, Alexa is confronted by her own mortality as she tries to unravel the secrets of a murdered man whose body has been found in someone else's grave. Detective thriller, starring Lucy Lawless and Bernard Curry

programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: An asylum seeker is murdered on a notorious Edinburgh housing estate in what appears to be a race-hate crime - but Rebus suspects there is something more complex behind it. A local MP also reports a missing Kosovan girl, who was last seen in a car with a known criminal - and it turns out he has vanished as well. Ken Stott stars as Ian Rankin's jaded detective, with Claire Price and Cora Bissett

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. A university student who has committed suicide is revealed to be the victim of bullying, and concern grows when a gun is discovered in his locker and a memory card in his stomach is found to contain photographs of his tormentors. The team's fears become a reality when a building comes under attack by a rampaging shooter, leaving Harry and Nikki trapped inside while a helpless Leo is forced to watch on CCTV as the dramatic events unfold. Guest starring Phyllis Logan

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Poultry farmer Andrew, restaurant manager Angela and performer Jack go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest. The trio get off to a cracking start sorting thousands of eggs on poultry farmer Andrew's smallholding in Berkshire. While performer Jack takes his new role a little too slowly, restaurant manager Angela finds herself in a total scramble. The next day, both Andrew and Jack struggle to memorise four menus as they prepare to serve 40 covers at the high-end Indian restaurant where Angela works. For their final day, both the poultry farmer and restaurant manager face their fears to perform in front of a 45-strong audience at top tourist attraction the London Dungeon. Narrated by Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham

With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, with the RAF College Band taking centre stage as the year's home service. Others featured include the Combined Bands of the Royal Cavalry of the Sultanate of Oman, the United States Air Force Honor Guard Drill Team, the Royal Marines Band Plymouth, the Band of the Royal Regiment of Scotland, the Central Band of the Czech Armed Forces and the Banda Monumental de Mexico, the Tattoo Dance Company and the Top Secret Drum Corps

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Guest panellists Jack Dee, Romesh Ranganathan, Gaby Roslin and Tinchy Stryder join team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack on the comedy show in which participants attempt to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves. Hosted by Rob Brydon

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide it's time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when they're living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as he's about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by fellow chefs Theo Randall and Sabrina Ghayour, and comedian Russell Howard joins in via video-link. Plus, the usual mix of BBC archive clips

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond celebrate heritage food, cooking roast loin of roe deer with potato rosti and root vegetables. They are joined by award-winning chef Lorna McNee from Gleneagles and weather presenter Judith Ralston, who brings along her favourite beer to accompany the food

programme.title: Live Women's Football
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 12.30pm). Live coverage of the match at Wembley Stadium, as 2019/20 Women's Super League champions Chelsea face runners-up City. The Londoners were given the title despite trailing their opponents by a point when the season was curtailed due to having a game in hand and a better points-per-game average, and the last meeting between the sides resulted in a thrilling 3-3 draw in Manchester six months ago

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages an old desk, a pink armchair, a rusty old bucket and an unwanted painting, handing them out for renovation before giving any profits to the owners

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Oxton, Birkenhead, coming up with designs for musician Gareth, who says his garden is in need of a total rethink

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous journalists take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Natasha Kaplinsky, Kevin Maguire, Dermot Murnaghan, Nigel Nelson, Cathy Newman, James O'Brien, Isabel Oakeshott and Ella Whelan

programme.title: Sully - Miracle on the Hudson (2016)
programme.desc: Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Tom Hanks as pilot Chesley 'Sully' Sullenberger, who rose to fame in January 2009 when he safely landed US Airways Flight 1549 in New York's Hudson River after the plane lost all engine power shortly after take-off - an event that captured the imagination of the world. Also starring Aaron Eckhart, Laura Linney and Anna Gunn

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Langridge family spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia. Abigail feels a new start would be great for the whole family, but her husband and son are not convinced

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Anita Manning leads the proceedings, with Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron helping the teams

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Blenheim Palace to explore its private chapel and the grounds of this World Heritage site, while Laura Wright joins an inclusive choir for a hymn singalong

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. Prior to the opener, these sides had met in this format on 15 occasions, with England winning 10 of those contests, Pakistan claiming victory on four occasions and the other match ending in a tie. However, it was the visitors who prevailed by nine wickets the last time they played a T20 at this venue in September 2016

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death', the Duke of Wellington's night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: A frightening visit from a potential client puts a new case on the table and Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott set to work looking into reports of a strangled child. With their detective agency thriving, the duo are also recruited to investigate the blackmail of a government minister, with Robin going undercover in the House of Commons. The return of the crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger, with Robert Glenister and Robert Pugh

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Stable manager Gemma, tea-room owner Frieda and commercial fisherman Pete compete, taking turns at doing one another's jobs before one of them walks away with the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle and Kate Humble find out how moving will encourage the secretary birds to mate, and serve a meal to a wolf. Jean Johansson learns how to determine a scorpion's gender

programme.title: Bears About the House
programme.desc: Part two of two. Conservationist Giles Clark continues his biggest mission to date - taking on the illegal wildlife trade and helping to build a new bear sanctuary in Laos, south-east Asia. At the sanctuary, Mary the sun bear is now nine months old. Now growing in confidence, she is becoming extremely determined, and picks up a skill unique to a sun bear, jumping. Later, the sanctuary receives some shocking news when they discover there has been a break-in, and the distraught team have to come to terms with a theft

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Seeing her daughter cope with cerebral palsy has inspired Bradford mum Nicola that a new life in Perth, Australia is the way forward. Nicki Chapman follows the family's progress

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts as team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Vianet Djenguet and Steve Backshall for another test of wildlife knowledge

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Regina warns Jack that Sir Richard is intending to use his friendship with Frank Gibbs to incite bigots in the election, while George reveals his intent to marry Sarah

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter T, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by heading to Thailand, where they discover why Bangkok has a reputation as the street food capital of the world, and help serve up school dinners. Heading out into the country's central plains, the duo join paddy-field workers and find out how rice is grown

programme.title: Animal Super Parents
programme.desc: A look at the fascinating behaviours exhibited by some of the natural world's most dedicated parents, beginning with a look at those animals that are tasked with raising their offspring alone. Examples include the slow loris covering her baby in toxic saliva, the Weddell seal putting her pup through winter boot camp, a weedy sea dragon camouflaging his eggs against his body and the giant Pacific octopus sacrificing herself for her brood. Narrated by Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Dover Castle in Kent, Jonathan Pratt and James Lewis scour the crowd to find objects to take off to auction. Jonathan discovers an elegant Lalique figure and James finds a pristine collection of Chinese snuff bottles. Presenter Paul Martin uncovers the murderous history of the castle

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Angellica Bell, Gyles Brandreth, Scarlett Moffatt and Iain Stirling go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Gardening Together with Diarmuid Gavin
programme.desc: Diarmuid helps design a garden in Co Kerry for Shane Auliffe, who wants to see a formal, grand garden from his window. He offers advice for gardeners with a bit of help from Mount Stewart head gardener Neil Porteous and shows how to transform a terrace area, as he makes the finishing touches to his own garden

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: TV presenters Anthea Turner and Andi Peters join experts Charles Hanson and Margie Cooper on a quest to find items to sell at auction. Their search takes them through Cheshire, Greater Manchester and Staffordshire, with Margie allowing Andi to indulge his passion for aviation when he swaps their classic car for a vintage Vulcan bomber, while Anthea learns about Staffordshire's rich heritage and why the county is synonymous with pottery

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Katie Derham presents the First Night of the live classical music festival, with a feast of performances including Beethoven's Third Symphony, Aaron Copland's Quiet City, and a new work by young British composer Hannah Kendall, all performed by the BBC Symphony Orchestra and BBC Singers, under conductor Sakari Oramo. Katie and special guest Stephen Fry also celebrate the return of live music to the Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From giant gooseberries and growing the perfect sweet pea to flamboyant dahlias and plants pollinated by a single human hair, Monty Don celebrates the plant passions of some of the extraordinary people that the programme has met over the last few years. He also has plenty of hints and tips of his own to keep enthusiastic gardeners busy over the weekend ahead

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom and Louise look back on their early love life, and try to decide if they can see a future together. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from the first T20 match between England and Pakistan at Old Trafford

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Nazanin Zaghari-Ratcliffe has been held prisoner in Iran for more than four years after being convicted of spying charges. Nobody knows how many other British citizens have been imprisoned by the Iranian authorities, because the UK government refuses to say. Reporter Darragh MacIntyre meets the families of some of those who have been arbitrarily detained and asks whether the payment of a historic debt could set them free.

programme.title: Cuba: Castro vs the World
programme.desc: Part two of two. A look at the rocky relationship between Castro and Bill Clinton in the wake of the end of the Cold War, as the loss of Soviet support proved devastating for Cuba's economy and its people, leading to mass immigration to Florida. The programme also examines how Castro assisted Hugo Chavez' rise to power in Venezuela in exchange for oil, and the transition of power from an ageing Castro to his younger brother. Featuring contributions from key figures in this political era, including Clinton himself

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Ben Shires, Katie Thistleton, Karim Zeroual, Hacker T Dog, Hank Zipzer, Myles Wheeler from Whoops I Missed the Bus and Bella Ramsey of The Worst Witch feature in this look back at some of the more outrageous moments on Marrying Mum and Dad over the past five series

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: A Halloween-themed edition of weird clips from the natural world, featuring brainless bats, a deer munching on a bird, zombie starfish and a frozen waterfall of blood

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends bounce around an underground trampoline park in Snowdonia

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Joshua, 11, and his younger brother Owen are great friends, but Owen has Downs syndrome, which means that a lot of his parents' time is taken up looking after him. Joshua loves DJ'ing and admires Radio 1's Annie Mac. Here, helpers make Joshua his very own DJ den

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and his team go in search of an animal that has eluded the wildlife enthusiast his entire life - number one jungle predator the jaguar. They then turn their attentions to the sea and the killer whale, witnessing its unique method of hunting

programme.title: Deadly 60 on a Mission
programme.desc: Steve Backshall and the team continue to island-hop across the Southern Ocean, beginning in the Falkland Islands where they encounter the rockhopper penguin and one of the area's fiercest underwater predators. They then endure four days of sailing through some of the roughest seas on Earth as they travel to the remote Bird Island off South Georgia, where they are met by hundreds of Antarctic fur seals and a wandering albatross

programme.title: Rise of the Continents
programme.desc: Iain Stewart examines Australia and reveals how its formation and development has affected everything from Aboriginal history to modern-day mining, and even the evolution of its unique wildlife. He travels to the Great Australian Bight to show how the country was once joined to Antarctica, and how the koala's big, round face and fluffy ears are a result of adaptations to the change of climate after the two land masses split

programme.title: Mr Peabody & Sherman (2014)
programme.desc: A super-intelligent talking dog invents a time machine and adopts a human boy to assist with his experiments. The curious youngster cannot resist meddling with the invention - with disastrous results - and the duo have to journey into the past to undo the damage done to the course of history. Animated comedy, with the voices of Ty Burrell and Max Charles

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson begins with her take on a classic yoghurt pot cake, which was inspired by a scribbled note she found in an old Italian kitchen. The food writer's niece and nephew are recruited to make - and enjoy - a sausage meatball supper, while a girls' night in sees her rustling up squid and prawns with marjoram and chilli. The show ends with Nigella's 'tiramisini' - a lighter, quicker version of a traditional tiramisu

programme.title: A Cook Abroad
programme.desc: Edinburgh chef Tony Singh travels to the state of Punjab to trace his family's roots and discover whether his mum's Punjabi cooking is authentic. He visits Amritsar during the festival of Diwali, receives an exclusive invitation to eat and cook with a maharajah and meets the Nihang, an order of nomadic Sikh warriors. Tony discovers how the Sikh religion and the Punjab's troubled past have created a food and culture like no other, and ends his epic adventure at his ancestral home in New Delhi with his 93-year-old uncle

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: A Yorkshire hill farming family that keeps sheep and cows diversifies by investing in 300 goats, which are sold not just for meat and dairy products, but also goat milk soap. In the Lake District, Matt and Dani Blair settle into their new farm while also awaiting the birth of their first child - but a mix-up with the measuring tape proves costly and an escapee calf causes havoc. Crofter Graham is forced to adapt his working routine after a serious injury, and a storm threatens a Northumberland couple's plans to build a new home for their prize bull

programme.title: Oz the Great and Powerful (2013)
programme.desc: An amoral circus illusionist is transported to a magical land, where he is mistaken for a powerful wizard prophesied to fall from the sky and save the realm from evil. His attempt to take advantage of the situation leads him into a dangerous quest involving three powerful witches. Fantasy adventure prequel to The Wizard of Oz, starring James Franco, Mila Kunis, Rachel Weisz and Michelle Williams

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: Documentary footage of the volcanic fires of Iceland and the creatures that make their home amid this seemingly inhospitable terrain. The film was shot during the country's most dramatic eruption in years, in which a huge volcano exploded under the icecap, melting billions of tonnes of ice

programme.title: The Millionaires' Holiday Club
programme.desc: The first of two programmes going inside the world of high-end luxury holidays, tailored for some of the UK's wealthiest jet-setters, from a £40,000 a week trip to Seychelles to a night in Mustique for £2,000. Party girl Jane went on 12 holidays last year and the first of 2016 is a cruise around the Caribbean islands, while fruit farmers Mervyn and Heather sample their seventh visit to one of the most prestigious hotels in Jamaica

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On a railway journey from the capital Palermo, through the ancient town of Agrigento and the port of Siracusa, to Europe's largest volcano, Mount Etna, Michael Portillo explores Sicilian life under the dictatorship. He also finds out how the dictator took on the Mafia and asks whether it is true that under Mussolini, the trains ran on time

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon attempts to bring ashore three German airmen who have bailed out and landed in a lake and intend to stay in their dinghy until nightfall. Pike suggests they shoot through the vessel and sink them, but Captain Mainwaring is all for fair play - until Warden Hodges gets in on the act. Classic comedy first shown in 1975, starring Arthur Lowe and Ian Lavender

programme.title: Royal History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: The historian examines how key moments in Britain's history have been manipulated and mythologised, beginning with a look at the founding myths of the Reformation. She argues that Henry VIII may have had deeper motives for bringing about a religious and political schism than his desire for a divorce, and in doing so, laid the foundations for our modern constitution and economic power as an empire. She also questions the monarch's supposed belief in Martin Luther's Protestant revolution

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig hosts the quiz with a difference, in which the questions are so difficult points are awarded for the answers she finds most interesting. The show has reached the letter R, and a rather Rude episode. Joining Sandi are John Barrowman, Phill Jupitus, Aisling Bea and regular panellist Alan Davies

programme.title: When Bob Marley Came to Britain
programme.desc: Documentary examining the reggae superstar's relationship with the UK, and how his universal message of One Love and unity helped inspire a generation of young black British people. The film also takes a revealing look at how Marley spent his time while he was in Britain - the houses he lived in, football kickabouts in Battersea Park, and visits to the UK's growing Rastafarian community, including secret gigs in the north of England

programme.title: Sounds of the 70s 2
programme.desc: Collection of archive performances by artists including Bob Marley and the Wailers, Susan Cadogan, the Specials, Ken Boothe, Steel Pulse, Dave & Ansell Collins, Althea & Donna and Janet Kay. Reggae was imported to the UK by immigrants from the West Indies, and became an established chart presence in the 1970s

programme.title: Edinburgh Nights
programme.desc: Kiri Pritchard-McLean and Lolly Adefope capture the spirit of Edinburgh and bring highlights of new shows and events from the Fringe, the Book Festival and the International Festival, which are happening within the limits of the Covid-19 lockdown. Drawing on archive performances, the programme also tells the story of how the city became host to the largest, most accessible, creative arts festival in the world, while contributors including Evelyn Glennie and Fiona Shaw explain what the Festivals mean to them

programme.title: Looper (2012)
programme.desc: In the future, a crime syndicate uses time travel to dispose of people without trace, sending them back 30 years to be killed by hitmen. One of the assassins employed by the Mob finds his latest target is himself as an older man, and both versions go on the run as they try to change the course of history. Sci-fi thriller, with Joseph Gordon-Levitt, Bruce Willis and Emily Blunt

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode selects his favourite moments from the series as he reaches the end of his alphabetical search of the cookery archives for recipes from some of the nation's best TV chefs

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are on the Somerleyton Estate in Suffolk to find out about an ambitious project aiming to turn East Anglia into a giant nature reserve. Adam Henson fears the worst for his winter barley as he gets harvesting

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: From giant gooseberries and growing the perfect sweet pea to flamboyant dahlias and plants pollinated by a single human hair, Monty Don celebrates the plant passions of some of the extraordinary people that the programme has met over the last few years. He also has plenty of hints and tips of his own to keep enthusiastic gardeners busy over the weekend ahead

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Brian Cunningham counts butterflies to better understand how to attract them to his garden, while George and Gill Anderson are in the allotment making the most of August's produce

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's German Bite
programme.desc: The chef embarks on a journey to unearth some hidden culinary gems, seeking out enticing fishing opportunities as he hunts for the best seafood from the north German coast. The trip also has a personal perspective for Rick as he travels to meet the German side of his family in the Rheingau

programme.title: Swallows and Amazons (1974)
programme.desc: Four youngsters go boating while on holiday in the Lake District where they encounter two more children with a boat of their own, and a rivalry develops over a series of escapades. Family adventure, based on Arthur Ransome's classic novel, starring Virginia McKenna, Ronald Fraser, Sophie Neville and Simon West

programme.title: The Lake District: A Wild Year
programme.desc: For the wildlife and people who live amid the stunning scenery of the Lake District, life is one of continuous change. This documentary features cutting-edge camera techniques offering a new and unique perspective on a turbulent year in the life of England's largest national park. Time-lapse photography shows months and weeks passing in seconds - snow and ice giving way to sunshine or the frequent rain showers - while the animals, plants and people find extraordinary ways to cope with the challenges of this ancient landscape. Narrated by Bernard Cribbins

programme.title: Rio Conchos (1964)
programme.desc: A shipment of rifles is stolen by a former Confederate general in the aftermath of the American Civil War, and he begins selling them to hostile Apaches. A cavalry major is assigned to bring back the weapons, aided by a mismatched band including one of his fellow officers, a duplicitous Mexican and a drunken former rebel. Western, starring Richard Boon and Stuart Whitman

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: An exploration of the history and culture of some of the most extreme locations in Britain. Nick Crane visits the Isles of Scilly, Ruth Goodman walks the dangerous path traversed daily by the Branscombe Cliff farmers in Devon, and Mark Horton investigates the Transatlantic Telegraph service. Meanwhile, Hermione Cockburn learns how the fossil of a large sea creature found at St David's Head, Pembrokeshire, shaped scientists' understanding of Earth's history

programme.title: The Millionaires' Holiday Club
programme.desc: The second of two programmes taking a glimpse into the world of high-end holidays. This edition follows Richard as he plans a big surprise for his long-time partner Martyn. Whether it's at their £10,000 a week private villa in Barbados or on the idyllic island of Mustique, the only challenge Richard now faces is picking the right moment to propose. Plus, Peter and wife Christine, who enjoy 'spending their childrens' inheritance' on exotic holidays, plan a trip to a top hotel in India, booking a suite once used by Prince Charles and the Duchess of Cornwall. Narrated by Eve Best

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: A final round-up of highlights from the last two series of the motoring show. Featuring the team embarking on a road trip across the highlands of Peru in classic American cars, and Chris Harris putting an all-electric race car through its paces

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An eco-friendly gift-wrap business gets a helping hand from Santa, and a sock entrepreneur embodies the holiday spirit with his buy-one-give-one business model. A pair of candle entrepreneurs also try to persuade the Dragons - Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones - that their products can spark some serious profit. But while all the hopefuls dream of Dragon cash for Christmas, these investors don't give the gift of investment unless a business has real sparkle. Presented by Evan Davis

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian presents a guide to medical dramas, looking back at shows including Emergency Ward 10, Dr Finlay's Casebook, Casualty, Angels and Doctors. Harry assesses each show for the authenticity of sets and costumes, celebrates some of the most frequently recurring tropes of the genre, and reveals what it takes to make a successful hospital drama

programme.title: Liverpool FC: The 30 Year Wait
programme.desc: Documentary on Liverpool's Premier League title success, the first time they had tasted top-flight glory since the 1989/90 First Division campaign. The Reds were all set to clinch the title in record time, but the enforced break due to the coronavirus put their celebrations on hold, until they were eventually crowned champions in June thanks to Manchester City's defeat to Chelsea. Featuring interviews with key players and staff, this documentary focuses on what it means for the title to have finally returned to Merseyside after so many years

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Nathan is horrified by what Isaac's painting depicts and, as the artist's ominous premonitions begin to manifest themselves at Claire's homecoming, Peter races against time to save the cheerleader from Sylar's insidious evil. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere and Masi Oka

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Niki's father pays her a visit which brings out her bad side, Hiro attempts to change the future of somebody close to him, and Claire joins the cheerleading squad in a six-month flashback to when the heroes discovered their powers. Fantasy drama, starring Ali Larter, Hayden Panettiere and Masi Oka

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A further selection of highlights from the stand-up show focusing on a different topic each week. This time around, Rob Brydon, Sara Pascoe, Dara O Briain, Lou Sanders, Rich Hall, Dane Baptiste and others offer their thoughts on sex

programme.title: Spaceship (2016)
programme.desc: A teenager whose mother died in mysterious circumstances fakes her own abduction by aliens. Her father, still consumed with grief after seven years, tries to discover what has happened to her by seeking out her friends - who harbour strange obsessions of their own. Drama, starring Alexa Davies and Antti Reini

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two people with dementia. Whilst out shopping with his wife, 83-year-old John turned his back for a moment, before realising that his 82-year-old wife Sheila has vanished on the busy streets of Gloucester town centre. After a frantic search around town, John calls Gloucestershire Police and a major search operation swings into action. Elsewhere in rural Gloucestershire, police scour lanes and wooded areas as they search for 79-year-old John, who disappeared from the home he shares with his wife, Marian when she was out visiting a family member for lunch

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: With spring well under way, the amateur cameraman should be filming badgers, but there is no sign of any cubs and the food he has put down is being eaten by squirrels, pheasants and rodents. Johnny invites Tony Thorpe to the hide, hoping he will bring luck - but forgets about his aversion to rats

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: The late 1960s saw the first regular transmission of colour television, and the music world followed the trend with the launch of BBC television show Colour Me Pop. This episode features some of the programme's highlights, including performances by the Who, the Kinks, the Small Faces, the Bonzo Dog Doo-Dah Band and the Jimi Hendrix Experience

programme.title: Big Hits - Top of the Pops 1964 to 1975
programme.desc: Memorable archive performances from 1964 through to 1975, representing a sample of the first 12 years of Top of the Pops. Among those featured are the Rolling Stones, Tom Jones, Status Quo, Procol Harum, Stevie Wonder, Queen, the Kinks, Stealers Wheel, Julie Driscoll, Peter Sarstedt and the Seekers

programme.title: Peter Green: Man of the World
programme.desc: The story of the blues performer, who found fame in the 1960s as lead guitarist with Fleetwood Mac, but left the public stage for years as he battled drug addiction and mental illness. Featuring archive footage and interviews with Noel Gallagher, Carlos Santana, Green's former bandmates and the musician himself

programme.title: Fleetwood Mac - Don't Stop
programme.desc: Documentary looking back over the band's long career, from the early blues outfit led by Peter Green in the late 1960s, to their reincarnation in the 1970s with the line-up that went on to record the 40-million-selling album Rumours. Mick Fleetwood, John McVie, Lindsey Buckingham and Stevie Nicks reveal the truth behind the successes, failures and relationships that characterised their music and made them one of the most enduring acts in popular music

programme.title: Foo Fighters at Reading 2019
programme.desc: Dave Grohl, Taylor Hawkins, Nate Mendel, Chris Shiflett, Pat Smear and Rami Jaffee bring their rock marathon master class back to headlining Reading, a tradition they began in 2002. Huw Stephens introduces highlights from the set, as the band run through classic hits from their back catalogue

programme.title: Peter Green: Man of the World
programme.desc: The story of the blues performer, who found fame in the 1960s as lead guitarist with Fleetwood Mac, but left the public stage for years as he battled drug addiction and mental illness. Featuring archive footage and interviews with Noel Gallagher, Carlos Santana, Green's former bandmates and the musician himself

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Sybil prepares for George's 50th birthday and the arrival of her sister and niece from England, while persistent rain means Martin's cattle are yet to be turned out of their winter sheds into the fields. Bobby and Anne hold an open day to educate the public about farming and John has to urgently call the vet when one of his heifers gets into difficulty calving

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin experiences life in the latter days of the Soviet Union as he travels from St Petersburg - then known as Leningrad - to the port of Odessa, mere days before the 1991 coup in Moscow

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The complex trail of clues finally lead the police to a man they believe could be the killer. The officers then wait to find out what his next move will be - until the case takes a brutal twist that has terrifying consequences for Martin and Saga. Thriller, in Danish and Swedish, starring Kim Bodnia and Sofia Helin

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The case reaches a terrifying conclusion as Martin realises he is the killer's final target. He joins his colleagues to search for the villain, but faces a race against time to prevent further bloodshed. Meanwhile, Saga refuses to be deterred from her pursuit of justice. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Thirteen months after the events of the first season, a coastal tanker leaves the Oresund waterway and is headed straight for the bridge. When the coastguards board the ship, they discover there is no crew, but five youths are found chained below deck. Crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Kim Bodnia

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: While Saga and Martin are quarantined, someone posts a video online of four individuals wearing masks and using signs to claim responsibility for the plague. This coincides with several reported deaths as a result of eating, among other things, poisoned apples. Scandinavian crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin, Kim Bodnia and Dag Malmberg

programme.title: Big Hits - Top of the Pops 1964 to 1975
programme.desc: Memorable archive performances from 1964 through to 1975, representing a sample of the first 12 years of Top of the Pops. programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Sybil prepares for George's 50th birthday and the arrival of her sister and niece from England, while persistent rain means Martin's cattle are yet to be turned out of their winter sheds into the fields. Bobby and Anne hold an open day to educate the public about farming and John has to urgently call the vet when one of his heifers gets into difficulty calving

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: The presenter examines how the car industry in Germany and Japan blossomed following the Second World War, becoming manufacturing giants that far outstripped the Allied nations that had defeated them. He test drives some of the vehicles Germany and Japan produced during the post-war period, as well as an assortment of other classic cars, including E-Types and Mustangs. He also reveals the sad tale of the Austin Allegro, a vehicle that had a devastating impact on the British car industry

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Sir Simon Rattle and the London Symphony Orchestra perform a packed programme, featuring star pianist Mitsuko Uchida playing Beethoven, works by much-loved British composers Elgar and Vaughan Williams, and a new composition by Thomas Adès. Suzy Klein presents from the Royal Albert Hall

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nick Crane heads to his beloved Western Isles in Scotland to attempt a daunting, long-coveted mountaineering challenge on the Isle of Skye

programme.title: Van Morrison - Up on Cyprus Avenue

programme.title: Van Morrison at the BBC
programme.desc: A vintage compilation of the multi-talented Northern Irish musician's most memorable studio performances

programme.title: When Bob Marley Came to Britain
Classic Albums
programme.desc: Concert footage, home movies and interviews in Jamaica give a background to the Wailers' LP Catch a Fire, which launched Bob Marley onto the global music scene. Record label boss Chris Blackwell reveals how he masterminded the overdubbing of the album for the rock market and Rabbit Brundrick (keyboards) and Wayne Perkins (electric guitar) tell how they were brought back in to add their parts to the songs

programme.desc: Family comedy based on the children's novel by David Walliams. Twelve-year-old Chloe leads a lonely existence - her mother is too busy with her political ambitions to take any interest in her daughter, her hen-pecked dad is hiding a secret of his own and she cannot hope to compete with her 'perfect' younger sister - to say nothing of the nasty girls who make her school life a misery. So when she meets a tramp in need of somewhere to stay, she invites him to hide in her family's garden shed - but it soon turns out there is more to her smelly guest than meets the eye. programme.desc: Tom, Eloushka, Lucas, Maddie and Madz travel to a massive sandwich factory in London where they make seven hundred thousand sandwiches a week. Adelie Foods has very high hygiene standards and productivity rates and their workers are disciplined and incredibly fast. Will our five being able to demonstrate the focus needed to succeed here? When the boss starts them off making a cheese and pickle ploughman's sandwich, it becomes clear that this work is considerably more difficult than they had anticipated. As they all compete for a promotion, who has the skill to keep up with the production lines and make sandwiches of the quality that their boss expects?

programme.desc: Mr Malone holds a mock trial to resolve a dispute Martha and Ollie are having over the ownership of Edison, a cuddly lightbulb they won together at a fairground hoopla. Rufus is appointed Ollie's ineffective lawyer. (His amenability is something his girlfriend Kat finds very frustrating). Lily is Martha's disappointing lawyer - whilst promising an epic opening statement, Lily makes it all about Rob being in America. Ollie asks Martha to prove she can throw a hoop on target. Martha objects, but Judge Kat insists that it will be amusing to witness Martha throw hoops. Martha applies the scientific calculations she gave to Ollie to help him win Edison, but she still fails to throw a hoop successfully. Ollie nears victory when the court adjourns because Mr Malone is hungry. Later, whilst Rufus admits to Kat that he doesn't feel he is cut out to be a lawyer, he offers Kat the largest slice of cake even though he wants it. This infuriates Kat. programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates go on an adventure to find the most unusual and amazing places in Asia. And this time it's all the Main Events in one show! They take part in a belly button festival, a giant sandal race and wooden washtub challenge in Japan; ride in tuk-tuk taxis in Sri Lanka; give it pedalo power and take part in a game of Kabaddi in India; test their Kung Fu talents in China; and show off their blowpipe skills at a tribal village in Malaysia! Ed is joined on this bonkers road trip by Cel Spellman, Chris Johnson, Naomi Wilkinson, Ben Shires, Michelle Ackerley, Victoria Cook, Johny Pitts and Inel Tomlinson - there's stuff to do in Asia that is totally ace and it turns up All Over The Place

programme.title: Show Me What You're Made Of
programme.desc: In episode four of Show Me What You're Made Of our teens they leave Cambodia to fly further east to the island of Taiwan. Here they head straight to an enormous tea plantation where they grow the tea for the British export market. It's been a tough trip for our five teens and within minutes of arriving at the tea factory one of them feels unwell. With a man down how will the other four cope with the very physical work involved? Their boss is the strictest one yet and he is not a man to be messed with. At the end of their first shift the boss proves that he is very serious about tea when he sacks one of the team. They may be shocked but can they pull together as a team to turn this situation around? Show Me What You're Made Of is presented by Stacey Dooley

programme.desc: Issie gets a new vision of Mystic running through the forest along an old railway track, and wonders if this might be the clue that will lead her to Blaze's herd. As they journey deeper into the forest, past their favourite swimming hole, the kids make a series of bewildering discoveries and eventually realise they're completely and utterly lost. With one of the gang's future ambitions at stake, they face a race against time to get back to the stables. Despite their best efforts to hide what happened, the consequences of their mission have serious consequences for them all as Tom presents the group with a difficult dilemma.
