programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle observes a Cuban crocodile death roll for the first time, while the keepers face a challenge from a pride of lairy lions and Jean Johansson meets some chatty parrots

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Properties in West Bromwich, Leeds and Croydon, all bought without viewing ahead of the auction, are sold under the hammer, and the owners reveal whether they regret their buys

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A 95-year-old navy veteran is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. Plus, the dental team has to take to the road to get to patients across Shetland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles is at the helm with experts Danny Sebastian and Caroline Hawley, as two teams scour the antiques centre in the hope that they can make the greater profit at auction. There are plenty of highs and lows in the saleroom, while Eric also gets the chance to meet an unlikely war hero

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Kam is schooled in privilege, and Leonie makes excuses to stick with Erika. Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke star

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Consumer journalists Jasmine Birtles, Chris Hollins, Simon Calder, Adam Pearson and Rebecca Wilcox compete in a charity edition hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is tasked with helping a couple and their young sons quit London to make a new life for themselves in Derbyshire, with a budget of £475,000. He also spends time with an artisan craftsman who is bringing back the local skill of knife-making

programme.title: Call That Hard Work?
programme.desc: Poultry farmer Andrew, restaurant manager Angela and performer Jack go head to head in a bid to prove their job is the hardest. The trio get off to a cracking start sorting thousands of eggs on poultry farmer Andrew's smallholding in Berkshire. While performer Jack takes his new role a little too slowly, restaurant manager Angela finds herself in a total scramble. The next day, both Andrew and Jack struggle to memorise four menus as they prepare to serve 40 covers at the high-end Indian restaurant where Angela works. For their final day, both the poultry farmer and restaurant manager face their fears to perform in front of a 45-strong audience at top tourist attraction the London Dungeon. Narrated by Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating acts as co-host alongside Alex Jones for the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, with the RAF College Band taking centre stage as the year's home service. Others featured include the Combined Bands of the Royal Cavalry of the Sultanate of Oman, the United States Air Force Honor Guard Drill Team, the Royal Marines Band Plymouth, the Band of the Royal Regiment of Scotland, the Central Band of the Czech Armed Forces and the Banda Monumental de Mexico, the Tattoo Dance Company and the Top Secret Drum Corps

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time he's pushing back the boundaries of science and discovering a world of arcade games, bionic councillors and Welsh rocket men, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian undertakes a work placement at the Welsh Mountain Zoo near Colwyn Bay, Conwy, where his tasks include looking after tigers, sea lions and monkeys. However, Rhod struggles to remember the exotic creatures' names, let alone how to take care of them

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide it's time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when they're living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as he's about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by fellow chefs Theo Randall and Sabrina Ghayour, and comedian Russell Howard joins in via video-link. Plus, the usual mix of BBC archive clips

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond celebrate heritage food, cooking roast loin of roe deer with potato rosti and root vegetables. They are joined by award-winning chef Lorna McNee from Gleneagles and weather presenter Judith Ralston, who brings along her favourite beer to accompany the food

programme.title: Live Women's Football
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 12.30pm). Live coverage of the match at Wembley Stadium, as 2019/20 Women's Super League champions Chelsea face runners-up City. The Londoners were given the title despite trailing their opponents by a point when the season was curtailed due to having a game in hand and a better points-per-game average, and the last meeting between the sides resulted in a thrilling 3-3 draw in Manchester six months ago

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Oxton, Birkenhead, coming up with designs for musician Gareth, who says his garden is in need of a total rethink

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards concludes his exploration of Welsh history by taking a look at modern-day Wales, including the role television has played in recent times. Plus, how a Welshman battled to set up one of Britain's most cherished institutions, the drowning of a valley to provide water for Liverpool, and the new generation of sporting greats who set flags waving

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous journalists take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Natasha Kaplinsky, Kevin Maguire, Dermot Murnaghan, Nigel Nelson, Cathy Newman, James O'Brien, Isabel Oakeshott and Ella Whelan

programme.title: Sully - Miracle on the Hudson (2016)
programme.desc: Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Tom Hanks as pilot Chesley 'Sully' Sullenberger, who rose to fame in January 2009 when he safely landed US Airways Flight 1549 in New York's Hudson River after the plane lost all engine power shortly after take-off - an event that captured the imagination of the world. Also starring Aaron Eckhart, Laura Linney and Anna Gunn

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Langridge family spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia. Abigail feels a new start would be great for the whole family, but her husband and son are not convinced

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Anita Manning leads the proceedings, with Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron helping the teams

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Blenheim Palace to explore its private chapel and the grounds of this World Heritage site, while Laura Wright joins an inclusive choir for a hymn singalong

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. Prior to the opener, these sides had met in this format on 15 occasions, with England winning 10 of those contests, Pakistan claiming victory on four occasions and the other match ending in a tie. However, it was the visitors who prevailed by nine wickets the last time they played a T20 at this venue in September 2016

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death', the Duke of Wellington's night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: A frightening visit from a potential client puts a new case on the table and Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott set to work looking into reports of a strangled child. With their detective agency thriving, the duo are also recruited to investigate the blackmail of a government minister, with Robin going undercover in the House of Commons. The return of the crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger, with Robert Glenister and Robert Pugh

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

With analysis by James McFadden and Chris Iwelumo

While performer Jack takes his new role a little too slowly, restaurant manager Angela finds herself in a total scramble. The next day, both Andrew and Jack struggle to memorise four menus as they prepare to serve 40 covers at the high-end Indian restaurant where Angela works. For their final day, both the poultry farmer and restaurant manager face their fears to perform in front of a 45-strong audience at top tourist attraction the London Dungeon. Narrated by Sunetra Sarker

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Stephanie Connell embark on the last leg of their journey. Steph tries her hand at playing the bagpipes in Hexham, Northumberland, while Phil finds out how coal mining led to the development of Britain's rail network. The duo then meet at the final auction in Darlington, Co Durham

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Ronan Keating acts as co-host alongside Alex Jones for the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Matter of Loaf and Death
programme.desc: The duo open their own bakery, Top Bun, and business is soon booming - not least because a 'cereal killer' is targeting all the rival bakers in town. Even better, Wallace has caught the eye of local beauty Piella Bakewell. But Gromit is more concerned that his master might become the killer's next victim. With the voices of Peter Sallis and Sally Lindsay

programme.title: The Royal Edinburgh Military Tattoo
programme.desc: Bill Paterson narrates highlights of the event held on the esplanade of Edinburgh Castle, with the RAF College Band taking centre stage as the year's home service. Others featured include the Combined Bands of the Royal Cavalry of the Sultanate of Oman, the United States Air Force Honor Guard Drill Team, the Royal Marines Band Plymouth, the Band of the Royal Regiment of Scotland, the Central Band of the Czech Armed Forces and the Banda Monumental de Mexico, the Tattoo Dance Company and the Top Secret Drum Corps

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: Following two sets of fans making a country music pilgrimage to Spain for a week in the company of the Three Amigos and a host of other big Irish stars of the scene. Having swapped the grey skies of home for the Spanish sun, they spend all day by the pool and all night jiving to the sound of their country heroes

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock decide it's time to move out of home and head to Spain to run a bar. First to receive the Spain news is Mairead, who is in disbelief - how is she going to keep digging them out of holes when they're living overseas? Billy Murphy receives unexpected information that causes him to faint - resulting in a large lump on his head and concern for Mairead and the lads. Then, Sergeant Healy arrives at the door with a barbershop quartet serenading Mairead as he's about to propose. Chris Walley, Alex Murphy and Hilary Rose star

programme.title: The Witches of Eastwick (1987)
programme.desc: Three single women dabble in magic to conjure up the perfect lover, and unintentionally summon Satan himself, who seduces them one by one. They come to realise the danger the lecherous devil presents and decide to end their relationships, but the infernal rogue does not take kindly to rejection. Fantasy comedy based on John Updike's novel, with Jack Nicholson, Cher, Michelle Pfeiffer and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by fellow chefs Theo Randall and Sabrina Ghayour, and comedian Russell Howard joins in via video-link. Plus, the usual mix of BBC archive clips

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond celebrate heritage food, cooking roast loin of roe deer with potato rosti and root vegetables. They are joined by award-winning chef Lorna McNee from Gleneagles and weather presenter Judith Ralston, who brings along her favourite beer to accompany the food

programme.title: Live Women's Football
programme.desc: Chelsea v Manchester City (Kick-off 12.30pm). Live coverage of the match at Wembley Stadium, as 2019/20 Women's Super League champions Chelsea face runners-up City. The Londoners were given the title despite trailing their opponents by a point when the season was curtailed due to having a game in hand and a better points-per-game average, and the last meeting between the sides resulted in a thrilling 3-3 draw in Manchester six months ago

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore salvages an old desk, a pink armchair, a rusty old bucket and an unwanted painting, handing them out for renovation before giving any profits to the owners

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich brothers are in Oxton, Birkenhead, coming up with designs for musician Gareth, who says his garden is in need of a total rethink

programme.title: Cinderella (2015)
programme.desc: An orphaned girl is treated as a servant by her cruel stepmother and her two daughters. Her life is changed for ever by the intervention of a kindly fairy godmother, who gives her the opportunity to attend a lavish royal ball and win the heart of a handsome prince. Romantic fantasy, starring Lily James, Cate Blanchett, Richard Madden and Helena Bonham Carter

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: The Wrong Trousers
programme.desc: Nick Park's Oscar-winning animation featuring the bumbling inventor and his faithful dog, whose lives are turned upside down after foolishly offering board and lodgings to villainous penguin Feathers McGraw. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous journalists take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. Taking part are Natasha Kaplinsky, Kevin Maguire, Dermot Murnaghan, Nigel Nelson, Cathy Newman, James O'Brien, Isabel Oakeshott and Ella Whelan

programme.title: Sully - Miracle on the Hudson (2016)
programme.desc: Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Tom Hanks as pilot Chesley 'Sully' Sullenberger, who rose to fame in January 2009 when he safely landed US Airways Flight 1549 in New York's Hudson River after the plane lost all engine power shortly after take-off - an event that captured the imagination of the world. Also starring Aaron Eckhart, Laura Linney and Anna Gunn

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: The Client (1994)
programme.desc: A Mafia lawyer tells a boy where a politician's body is buried shortly before committing suicide, prompting a team of district attorneys to press the young witness for information. However, the streetwise kid refuses to give evidence unless his own and his family's safety is guaranteed, and hires a lawyer to ensure his demands are met. Courtroom thriller based on the novel by John Grisham, with Susan Sarandon, Tommy Lee Jones and Brad Renfro

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Liverpool v Arsenal. Highlights of the curtain-raiser to the top-flight campaign, held at Wembley Stadium. The Reds secured their place in this match by winning their first title of the Premier League-era, while the Gunners defeated Chelsea 2-1 in the FA Cup final and prevailed 2-1 at Emirates Stadium when the sides last met

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Langridge family spend a trial week living in Perth, Australia. Abigail feels a new start would be great for the whole family, but her husband and son are not convinced

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: A mother and son take on a mother and daughter at the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh. Anita Manning leads the proceedings, with Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron helping the teams

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits Blenheim Palace to explore its private chapel and the grounds of this World Heritage site, while Laura Wright joins an inclusive choir for a hymn singalong

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore rescues two items from a recycling centre in Witley, Surrey, with creative duo Josh and Oli coming up with an idea that could make a fantastic profit

programme.title: Live International T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Isa Guha presents all the action from the second T20 in the three-match series, taking place at Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. Prior to the opener, these sides had met in this format on 15 occasions, with England winning 10 of those contests, Pakistan claiming victory on four occasions and the other match ending in a tie. However, it was the visitors who prevailed by nine wickets the last time they played a T20 at this venue in September 2016

programme.title: Wallace & Gromit: A Grand Day Out
programme.desc: Nick Park's Bafta-winning film which introduced the animated inventor and his faithful canine sidekick to the world. Wallace is distraught when his cheese supplies run out, so he decides to take a trip to the moon. All he needs to do is build himself a rocket. With the voice of Peter Sallis

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition, food writer Mary Berry takes over as guest editor to reveal how farming and the countryside have influenced her life and career - and still do. Matt Baker is with her to discover what has inspired her, from early childhood on her parents' smallholding to the rural issues she holds dear today. Adam Henson investigates the state of British pig farming, while Anita Rani visits a school with a field-to-fork ethos, which is something Mary would love to see more of

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Cromer pier in Norfolk, where items brought in to be valued include a strange machine labelled 'Certain Death', the Duke of Wellington's night cap, a note related to Oliver Cromwell, and letters from both Queen Victoria and John Lennon. Plus, Fiona is challenged to guess the values of three rare pieces of antique jewellery

programme.title: Strike
programme.desc: A frightening visit from a potential client puts a new case on the table and Cormoran Strike and Robin Ellacott set to work looking into reports of a strangled child. With their detective agency thriving, the duo are also recruited to investigate the blackmail of a government minister, with Robin going undercover in the House of Commons. The return of the crime thriller based on books by JK Rowling, starring Tom Burke and Holliday Grainger, with Robert Glenister and Robert Pugh

programme.title: Van Morrison - Up on Cyprus Avenue
programme.desc: Highlights of Van Morrison's unique live performance recorded on his 70th birthday in August 2015, on the Belfast avenue he made famous through the iconic album Astral Weeks

programme.title: The Accountant (2016)
programme.desc: High-functioning autistic number-cruncher Christian Wolff moonlights as a money launderer for the mob. However, the Treasury Department start closing in on him, so he decides to take on a legitimate client in the form of a robotics company. As he works through their books, he uncovers a discrepancy involving millions of dollars. Thriller, starring Ben Affleck, Anna Kendrick and JK Simmons

programme.desc: The coronavirus pandemic has presented humanity with an almighty shock. Our evermore interconnected and technologically advanced societies are now in lockdown and we are fearful for our health and economic futures thanks to an invisible virus. Stephen Sackur speaks to the Israeli historian and best-selling author Yuval Noah Harari. What 21st century lesson can we draw from the spread of Covid-19? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Tuesday 28 April.

programme.desc: In times of crisis, we learn about who we really are, and so it is that the global coronavirus pandemic is revealing truths about humankind and how we balance self and collective interest. Stephen Sackur speaks to writer and historian Rutger Bregman whose book Humankind: A Hopeful History is making waves around the world. programme.title: 'San Fhuil (It's in the Blood)
programme.desc: A personal portrait of a South Uist crofting township where the Greylag geese outnumber people by three to one. Over the last 30 years, the resident population of Greylags has increased from less than 1,000 to over 9,000 birds. With little to check the breeding Greylag, the geese are destroying the cultivation of Uist corn

programme.title: Druthag na Hearadh (A Little Drop of Harris)
programme.desc: With visitor numbers exceeding all expectations, and sales of Harris gin hugely surpassing initial forecasts, the team members work hard to keep up. However, it's not all taking place inside the distillery, Peter the Retail Manager is curious about where peat, used in the whisky making, comes from. Domhnall, one of the distillers, happily obliges and takes him out to the site of his grandfather's peat banks. As the distillate has been tweaked and optimised, the first private cask is filled by Anderson Bakewell, founder and chairman of the distillery, and in what seems like the blink of an eye the first year anniversary is upon them

programme.desc: It's a very special time for Sweeny on the croft as he welcomes the first calf to be born on the croft since his grandfather was a crofter

programme.desc: Javier Rincon and Professor Donald Yool perform complex surgery on a dog, Joanna Jackson is busy with lambing and Romain Pizzi examines Mo the gibbon

programme.desc: Donneil struggles to contain his guilt while Alasdair bumps into Claire at the prison. Màiri plans to meet Isla at Pàdruig's house, but there's no sign of Isla

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: A life-threatening accident strikes Camus. Màiri is desperate for the truth and is willing to put her own life in danger in order to get justice for her father. Peigi receives some visitors in Glasgow

programme.title: The Sandyford Murder - Sgeulachd Jessie McLachlan

programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers visit Sheffield, where a dog whistle sends Mark Stevens barking mad. Corrie Jeffery stands resolute when a seller haggles hard to get her cash, while Jan Keyne has a belter of a diamond ring from India

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Hertfordshire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Victoria gets sad news, Belle grapples with her guilt when she sees Moira struggling and Tracy asks more questions

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny tries to win Jenny round assuring her that he needed time alone and it won't happen again, but Scott tells Johnny it is about time they had a proper chat. Debbie senses Abi's reluctance to spend time with her, and Daniel encounters Nicky while she is out with a client

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when the body of a wildlife ranger is found on a remote island off the coast of Northumberland. Her death is at first assumed to be an accident, since she was alone on the island when she died, but marks on the body seem to point to murder. As Vera delves into the dead woman's personal life, she uncovers dark secrets and old resentments

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Hellboy II: The Golden Army (2008)
programme.desc: The demon superhero, aided by a team from the Bureau of Paranormal Research and Defence, leads the fight against renegade elf Prince Nuada who is intent on reawakening an unstoppable robot army and bringing about the demise of mankind. Action fantasy sequel directed by Guillermo del Toro, starring Ron Perlman, Selma Blair, Doug Jones and Luke Goss

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: The five remaining acts compete for a place in the final while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he is housesitting for Weasel. When he sees him returning from the grocery store with Fizz, he thinks they're burglars!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are at the supermarket buying ingredients to make the world's best cake. They bump into Magoo, who thinks he's at a carnival!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks that he is lost in a pyramid but he is in fact in a big conference room where Fizz is about to perform a stand-up act.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: International Rescue scrambles all the flight-capable Thunderbirds to rescue the crew of CIR.R.U.S, a high-atmosphere weather station, now in danger due to the arrival of a fierce storm. TB1, TB2 and TB3 are quickly crippled by the conditions, and the only hope of salvaging the mission lies with TB5 - and John's ability to mount his most daring rescue ever

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Kids' documentary series looking at what it takes to become a professional at all kinds of exciting jobs. Agent Kym has a really cool task for her latest mission from the Agency - she's at an ice sculpting factory.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: When Magoo treats Mr Cat to a parachute jump, the two friends land on the top of the AeroFizz. Mr Magoo mistakes it for a desert island!

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Weasel (in an animal costume) for a groundhog being hunted, and confiscates the hunter's gun.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is at the pool, he wants to start sub-marine hairdressing! But Magoo, thinks he is fishing in a lake, and starts trying to catch Weasel.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Fluffy decides to annoy Mike while he is in the middle of a yoga session with Cindy, causing Mike to overreact.

programme.title: Buck & Buddy
programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A fun run gives Mr Bean the chance to take revenge on the man who stole his parking space - but he has to hire a taxi to do it. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: The Ice Emperor summons an enormous ice Behemoth to attack Lloyd and his faithful wolf companion.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Ben and Gwen look for the infamous Bojamboo.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Dermot falls asleep while watching a classic Western. He dreams of those nearest and dearest to him, but they have all transformed into characters in a nightmarish Western drama.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Lulu decides it's do or die at the school dance. If Dermot doesn't kiss her, she'll kiss him. Meanwhile, Frankie discovers she must cure Mum and Dad before they transform fully into flesh-eating zombies.

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer Billy Ocean and chefs Stephen Terry and Daniel Clifford, as he serves barbecued aubergine, a spiced pineapple tarte tatin, and sole Veronique

programme.title: Mannequin (1987)
programme.desc: A struggling artist gets a job as a window dresser for a department store, only to discover one of his dummies is a reincarnated Egyptian princess who comes to life at night. As he falls for the living doll, she inspires him to transform the shop's display and make it the most spectacular in town. Fantasy comedy, starring Andrew McCarthy and Kim Cattrall

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of racing from Goodwood, including the 3.35 Celebration Mile. Plus, key races from Newmarket and Windsor. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports from Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: Nick Cannon hosts the final of the celebrity singing contest, with the three remaining mystery stars to be crowned this year's winner. As ever, regular judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger will be on hand to assess the performances and try to guess who is hiding behind the elaborate costumes

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the fourth episode, Stephen is joined by guests comedians Jimmy Carr and Crissy Rock, and Diversity star Jordan Banjo

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the grand final of the junior version of the talent show. Over the past eight weeks, the acts have been whittled down to four finalists, one chosen by each of the four members of the judging panel. Tonight, these acts compete to impress both their mentors, Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am, and the viewing public for the chance to be named this year's Voice Kids champion. The winner will receive a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: GoldenEye (1995)
programme.desc: James Bond heads for Russia to investigate the connection between the theft of an experimental helicopter and an orbital satellite weapon with the power to devastate entire cities. He discovers both have fallen into the hands of a mysterious criminal mastermind with a grudge against Britain and a connection with 007's past. Spy adventure, with Pierce Brosnan taking on his first assignment as 007. Sean Bean, Izabella Scorupco and Famke Janssen also star. Directed by Martin Campbell, who later helmed Daniel Craig's first outing as Bond, Casino Royale

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Airside officer Ian has a close shave out on the runway. Meanwhile, veteran Border Force officers Glen and Amarjit hunt down smuggled cargo

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage one of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 156km route from Nice Moyen Pays to Nice. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Small Fortune
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Dermot O'Leary, in which a trio of siblings takes on challenges in miniature settings to win a cash prize of up to £150,000

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz has dressed Weasel up as a supervillain in order to pretend to save the city. Magoo mistakes his disguised neighbour for an actress in danger.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Weasel accidentally launches Fizz next to Magoo. The latter, believing he is driving through a wildlife reserve, mistakes Fizz for a lost child.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's underwater scooting and has discovered Atlantis. In fact, he's in New York, riding Fizz's latest invention.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A heavy metal extraction platform carrying toxic waste at the bottom of the ocean experiences a devastating fire. Gordon rushes in with Thunderbird 4 when the rolling factory damages its steering capabilities. With the help of Lady Penelope and Parker on the surface investigating the dealings of dubious executive Hector Ambrose, boss of the company Hydrexla, Gordon sets about rescuing platform operator Ned Tedford and his plant Gladys and prevent the rig from losing its toxic cargo

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Kids' documentary series looking at what it takes to become a professional at all kinds of exciting jobs. Agent Danny becomes a news photographer and it's his mission to take the perfect photograph to appear in a newspaper.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Things take a very unusual turn after the turtles are struck by lightning during a storm.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike gets an idea of how he could impress Cindy after playing with Louise and her doll - he's going to stage a rescue mission.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A hungry stork moves in with Bean and starts eating him out of house and home. His solution is to return it to the wild - but first he has to teach it to fly

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson visits 'Adrenaland Jr.'

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Melvin Odoom, Una Healy and Luke Goss join Martin and Roman Kemp for the entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the grand final of the junior version of the talent show. Over the past eight weeks, the acts have been whittled down to four finalists, one chosen by each of the four members of the judging panel. Tonight, these acts compete to impress both their mentors, Paloma Faith, Danny Jones, Pixie Lott and Will.i.am, and the viewing public for the chance to be named this year's Voice Kids champion. The winner will receive a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the fourth episode, Stephen is joined by guests comedians Jimmy Carr and Crissy Rock, and Diversity star Jordan Banjo

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.title: The Living Daylights (1987)
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Back in April, as the country went into lockdown, Ant and Dec presented this year's Britain's Got Talent auditions, knowing that the finals would have to be postponed until later in the year. Now the series finally returns, as judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams decide which acts will make it through to the semi-finals

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Detective
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents a countdown of the top 25 TV detectives of all time, as voted for by the British viewing public. From Marple to Magnum PI, Luther to Line of Duty and Cracker to Columbo, Sheridan takes viewers on a whistle-stop trip through 70 years of the most famous sleuths on television. From the spires of Oxford with Morse to the skyscrapers of New York with Cagney & Lacey, and from the quintessentially British Midsomer Murders to the brooding Scandi Noir of The Bridge, the programme flips through the crime files of the greatest detectives that have ever graced TV screens

programme.title: Fast & Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaw's older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage two of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a mountainous 186km route from Nice Haut Pays to Nice. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Donington Park, with racing from Round Two of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and round two of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents action from the second round of the campaign, which came from Snetterton. Scott Redding was the standout performer at the 2019 race staged on this circuit, as he took the chequered flag in both races. The Englishman won 11 races overall last season, as he went on to win the riders' championship by just five points over Australia's Josh Brookes

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and his dealers visit Sheffield, where a dog whistle sends Mark Stevens barking mad. Corrie Jeffery stands resolute when a seller haggles hard to get her cash, while Jan Keyne has a belter of a diamond ring from India

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five friends from Hertfordshire answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Warwick Davis hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Victoria gets sad news, Belle grapples with her guilt when she sees Moira struggling and Tracy asks more questions

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Johnny tries to win Jenny round assuring her that he needed time alone and it won't happen again, but Scott tells Johnny it is about time they had a proper chat. Debbie senses Abi's reluctance to spend time with her, and Daniel encounters Nicky while she is out with a client

programme.title: Vera

The winner will receive a £30,000 bursary towards their musical education and a VIP holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: Rolling in It
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a game show featuring a giant arcade machine, which three members of the public have to play to win thousands of pounds. In the fourth episode, Stephen is joined by guests comedians Jimmy Carr and Crissy Rock, and Diversity star Jordan Banjo

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: Julia chooses six of her favourite and most accessible family treks from across the UK, in search of the perfect scenic walk that anyone can manage. She begins by heading to one of the most famous landmarks on the Jurassic Coast - Old Harry Rocks in Dorset, which boasts spectacular views towards the Isle of Wight and of the offshore chalk stacks, good opportunities for bird and butterfly watching and a rich variety of wild flowers in spring and summer. There are also miles of golden sandy beaches and a dramatic vista of the bay

programme.title: The Living Daylights (1987)
programme.desc: James Bond crosses the continents to help a KGB agent to defect to the West. While protecting him from an unknown assassin, the super-spy is drawn into the world of arms dealing and a plot to trade millions of pounds' worth of diamonds for weapons to supply mercenaries around the world. Action adventure, with Timothy Dalton making his debut appearance as 007, alongside Maryam d'Abo, Joe Don Baker and Art Malik

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The sleuth helps an old friend find refuge at country house Greenshaw's Folly, but a sinister shadow falls over the strange building's various inhabitants and a murderer strikes. Miss Marple uncovers secrets from the past about Miss Greenshaw as a dangerous storm gathers and the bodies start to pile up. Julia McKenzie heads an all-star cast that includes Jim Moir (better known as Vic Reeves), Kimberley Nixon, Fiona Shaw, Robert Glenister, Julia Sawalha, Judy Parfitt and Matt Willis (formerly of pop group Busted)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Back in April, as the country went into lockdown, Ant and Dec presented this year's Britain's Got Talent auditions, knowing that the finals would have to be postponed until later in the year. Now the series finally returns, as judges Simon Cowell, Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon and David Walliams decide which acts will make it through to the semi-finals

programme.title: Britain's Favourite Detective
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith presents a countdown of the top 25 TV detectives of all time, as voted for by the British viewing public. From Marple to Magnum PI, Luther to Line of Duty and Cracker to Columbo, Sheridan takes viewers on a whistle-stop trip through 70 years of the most famous sleuths on television. From the spires of Oxford with Morse to the skyscrapers of New York with Cagney & Lacey, and from the quintessentially British Midsomer Murders to the brooding Scandi Noir of The Bridge, the programme flips through the crime files of the greatest detectives that have ever graced TV screens

programme.title: Fast & Furious 7 (2015)
programme.desc: After defeating Owen Shaw and his crew and securing amnesty for their past crimes, Dom, Brian and the rest of the team return home to live normal lives again. However Shaw's older brother Deckard - a rogue special forces assassin vows revenge on Dom, who he holds responsible for leaving Owen in a coma. Action thriller sequel, starring Vin Diesel, Jason Statham and Paul Walker in his last film role

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage two of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a mountainous 186km route from Nice Haut Pays to Nice. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Donington Park, with racing from Round Two of the Ginetta GT4 Supercup and round two of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.title: British Superbike Championship Highlights
programme.desc: James Haydon presents action from the second round of the campaign, which came from Snetterton. Scott Redding was the standout performer at the 2019 race staged on this circuit, as he took the chequered flag in both races. The Englishman won 11 races overall last season, as he went on to win the riders' championship by just five points over Australia's Josh Brookes

programme.title: Spectre (2015)
programme.desc: James Bond goes rogue after receiving a cryptic message that sets him on the trail of a secretive criminal network. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa wants Martin to engage more with James, so she arranges for him to take their baby to playgroup, where he has to pair up with a mother and daughter and sing songs. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Obsessive-compulsive Mrs Tishell returns to the village, but her affection for Martin quickly disappears when he refuses to pass her fit for work. As if he didn't have enough on his plate, the grumpy doctor must also face his fear of blood when tests show that Lorna has a high level of ferritin, which is placing a strain on her heart and liver. Meanwhile, PC Penhale hopes to be selected for an elite police squad by taking part in a survival training course - but ends up shooting himself in the foot

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: Bradfield sees a chance to get revenge on Clifford and diverts the team to carry out surveillance on the Bentleys - and when Tennison learns of the operation, she is forced to make a split-second decision as to what is most important, her career or her family? As Bradfield uncovers more details about the heist, he faces the disapproval of his colleagues as he becomes determined to catch them in the act

programme.title: Prime Suspect 1973
programme.desc: The police officers are numb after the explosion, and as they search the rubble, they make a horrifying realisation. The Bentleys make one last stand as a manhunt is launched for Clifford and David, while a distraught Renee is put under arrest. Before long Tennison makes one final discovery about Julie Ann's murder - but as she gets closer to solving the case, she finds herself in mortal danger. Stefanie Martini stars as the novice WPC who would eventually become the tough senior detective made famous by Helen Mirren

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: Ghosts from the past return to haunt Morse as he investigates the apparent suicide of a terminally ill Oxford don whose widow turns out to be the woman the detective was set to marry in his youth. As the case develops, Lewis worries his partner's professionalism is being undermined. Starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Joanna David, Samantha Bond, David Haig, Adrian Dunbar and James Grout

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The murder of a well-known business tycoon leads Morse to investigate the man's feuding family, and inadvertently makes him the unwitting target of a hate campaign by the tabloids - a problem compounded by his uneasy relationship with a new chief superintendent. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star alongside Gwen Taylor, Martin Clunes, Alun Armstrong, Anna Massey, Charlotte Coleman and Rupert Graves

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The murder of a journalist has chilling echoes of a 1960s horror film starring Midsomer's own Stella Harris. When the actress's more famous sister Diana returns after a 40-year family rift, the body count rises, with each crime reminiscent of a movie plot - giving Barnaby and Jones what looks like a disturbing case of life imitating art. Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes star, with Harriet Walter and Sinead Cusack

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are mystified by the murders of a popular couple who were stabbed to death while they slept. Guest starring Samuel West

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team struggles to understand the involvement of 12-year-old Kaden Blake in the shooting of mother and carer Lia Brown. As a victim of gang culture, the boy's only protection is keeping silent, and by speaking out about the crime, he sets in motion a devastating chain of events. Guest starring Lucy Speed (EastEnders, The Bill), with Bradley Walsh and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: DS Sam Casey, a young officer with a strong sense of justice, is brought in to investigate the shooting of Devlin outside the Old Bailey, and is surprised to learn the gunman was specifically targeting police officers. The case sees the whole team emotionally spent, with the trial itself at risk of being derailed by a politically driven defence lawyer (Colin Salmon) with an axe to grind. Crime drama, starring Bradley Walsh, Freema Agyeman and Paul Nicholls, who joins the cast

programme.desc: Victoria gets sad news, Belle grapples with her guilt when she sees Moira struggling and Tracy asks more questions

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Richard Abernethie is found dead shortly after disinheriting his sole heir and specifying that his legacy is to be divided equally among the remaining family members. Poirot is called in by his solicitor friend Gilbert Entwhistle, to ascertain whether the late Mr Abernethie met with foul play - and also finds time to investigate the murder of a beneficiary under the new terms of the will. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Robert Bathurst, Anna Calder-Marshall and Geraldine James

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) and four known killers are among those who meet Poirot at a bridge evening arranged by wealthy adventurer Shaitana, who sits out the first game - only to be found later stabbed through the heart. As nobody has left or entered the victim's apartment during the night, the guests combine their skills to discover which of the party is responsible. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Lesley Manville and Alex Jennings

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of four races from Goodwood, including the 3.35 Supreme Stakes. Plus, three races from Perth, including the bet365 Gold Cup. With analysis from Jason Weaver and Mick FitzGerald, reports by Rishi Persad and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: As the Ropers settle into their new home, George realises he is going to have to look for a job. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: The Great St Trinian's Train Robbery (1966)
programme.desc: A crook and his cronies stash the money from a train heist in a deserted old mansion, planning to recover it in good time. However, several years later, when the thieves arrive to collect their loot, they discover the building has been turned into a school for girls where the pupils have become infamous for their escapades. Comedy, starring Frankie Howerd, Dora Bryan, Reg Varney and George Cole

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Princess Diana: A Life After Death
programme.desc: Diana, Princess of Wales' death set the British nation thinking about the monarchy, and a strange atmosphere existed throughout the nation in the unstable summer of 1997. Prime minister Tony Blair had just won an election with a landslide majority and the tabloid press were pursuing Diana everywhere. Events were already leading to a full-scale crisis and the sudden and unexpected death of Diana changed the country

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: Foundlings are people who were abandoned as babies, often in the first hours and days of their lives and have had no way to unlock the secrets of their past. In this 90-minute special presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell, new DNA technology and painstaking detective work is combined to help solve the mysteries of people with unknown beginning. The team uses an extraordinary new technique to try to identify birth families and find answers for people who have spent decades striving to find out where they came from

programme.title: Sanditon
programme.desc: Andrew Davies's adaptation of Jane Austen's unfinished final novel about a spirited young woman who finds herself in a seaside resort on the cusp of dramatic change - and becomes involved in the scheming of its ambitious inhabitants. Charlotte and Sidney find themselves at loggerheads but must work together in an attempt to rescue Miss Lambe. Tom tries to save Sanditon and his marriage, while Edward and Clara enter into a scandalous pact to finally get their hands on Lady Denham's inheritance

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky masters the fine art of living well on nothing a year, proving that a respectable public appearance is all you need to get credit. Her flirtation with Lord Steyne at least provides a reliable source of income, but the Sedley family are less fortunate. With her finances in tatters, a devastated Amelia is forced to send her son to live with the Osbournes. Becky rises to the very top of high society, even securing a royal audience - but Steyne expects something in return for his patronage, and when Rawdon is accosted by bailiffs, he seizes his chance

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Ronnie and Sam are given 10 hours to find a hostage who was taken by two armed men, following a bungled robbery in which an innocent 'have-a-go-hero' was shot dead. When one of the accused is seized, the team is offered a deal - he will tell them the location of the captive in return for immunity from prosecution. Tamzin Outhwaite guest stars as uncompromising lawyer Miriam Pescatore

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks is forced to reopen a 14-year-old case when a dying crack addict confesses to the murder of teenager Amanda Bennet, a crime for which her father Simon (guest star Tim McInnerny) was originally convicted. Brooks becomes torn between the belief of his former partner Bernie Rawlins and mounting doubts over the ability of current colleague Casey, and the case unearths the possibility that more ghosts from his drinking days will come back to haunt him. Starring Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Azores in the mid-Atlantic to investigate how animals cope with atmospheric pressure changes underwater, particularly the ones that have to take in air before they submerge. She swims with a school of dolphins and finds out about the inbuilt response that both animals and humans have when they go underwater, and meets a world champion freediver who teaches her the techniques needed to swim alongside sperm whales, before trying to overcome her nerves and dive down to the ocean floor on a single breath

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid gets in a flap when he learns his employer is planning to visit him at home. Family comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland
